title: expire_stale_sessions_before: negative max_age_secs mass-expires all sessions
description: Trigger: call expire_stale_sessions_before(now_ts, -86400) — e.g. a sign error at a new call site, or passing a pre-negated value. Actual behavior: cutoff = now_ts - (-86400) = now_ts + 86400 (a future timestamp). SQL condition updated_at < future matches every row ever written. All non-terminal sessions transition to Expired immediately. The type i64 accepts negative values silently; the old cutoff: i64 API required the caller to compute the cutoff explicitly and would produce a future cutoff only with a deliberate sign error. The new API hides the arithmetic inside the function, making the sign error less visible. Fix: change max_age_secs to u64 (a duration cannot be negative). Compute: cutoff = now_ts.saturating_sub(max_age_secs as i64) or add an anyhow::bail! if max_age_secs == 0 is also nonsensical.
close_reason: Fixed: max_age_secs: i64 → u64; cutoff computed via now_ts.saturating_sub(max_age_secs as i64)
